description: SEBI enforcement order concerning the India Electronic Development Fund (IEDF), an SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Fund focused on the electronics and semiconductor sector.

title: SEBI Order in the Matter of Bajaj Hindusthan Sugar Limited - Fund Diversion and RPT Non-Disclosure
description: SEBI order pursuant to Bombay High Court directions concerning Bajaj Hindusthan Sugar Limited, its promoters Shishir Bajaj and Kushagra Bajaj, involving alleged diversion of over ₹1,189 crore and non-disclosure of related party transactions.

title: SEBI Order Against Future Retail Limited Directors for Fraud, Misleading Disclosures and Fund Diversion
description: SEBI passes a 222-page enforcement order against 11 noticees including Kishore Biyani and other top executives of Future Retail Limited for misleading disclosures, understatement of debt, non-disclosure of related party transactions, and diversion of company funds.

title: SEBI Order on Front Running by Prijesh Kurani and Others
description: SEBI issued an enforcement order against 7 noticees including Prijesh Kurani and associates for front running trades of big client Anustup Trading Private Limited during January 2022 to February 2023.

title: SEBI Order Against Unregistered Investment Advisors Running Telegram Channels Intraday Jackpot and Professional Day Trading Institute
description: SEBI issues enforcement order against Akshay Kumar and five others for operating unregistered investment advisory and research analyst services through Telegram channels, collecting fees and making fraudulent profit claims.

title: Final Order Against Arun Panchariya in GDR Issue by Winsome Yarns Ltd.
description: SEBI issues a final adjudication order against Arun Panchariya for his role in fraudulent Global Depository Receipt (GDR) transactions by Winsome Yarns Ltd., following remand by SAT to recompute the penalty quantum.

title: Order Against M/s AFCO Capital India Private Limited - Merchant Banker Restrictions
description: SEBI issues order against AFCO Capital India Private Limited (SEBI Reg. No. INM000012555) for violations of Merchant Bankers Regulations, Intermediaries Regulations, PFUTP and PIT Regulations following inspection for April 2022 to August 2024.

title: Order in the matter of Front-running trades of Big Client - Sarvottam Securities Private Limited by multiple entities
description: SEBI order against four individuals — Mr. Sunny Bhatia, Mrs. Surbhi Chopra Bhatia, Mrs. Mamta Rani, and Mr. Kumar Ashok — for front-running trades of Big Client Sarvottam Securities Pvt. Ltd. in equity derivatives during January 2018 to December 2022.

title: SEBI Order Against Retro Green Revolution Limited - Price Manipulation and Market Fraud
description: SEBI issues enforcement order against 21 noticees including promoters and associates of Retro Green Revolution Limited for price manipulation, volume creation, and fraudulent trading activities on BSE.

title: Order Against Cash Cow Broking & Advisory Solutions for Unregistered Investment Advisory Activities
description: SEBI passes directions against Cash Cow Broking & Advisory Solutions and its partners for conducting investment advisory services without registration, ordering refund of fees collected from investors.

title: SEBI Order Against Arcotech Limited: Fund Diversion and Financial Statement Misrepresentation
description: SEBI issues enforcement order against Arcotech Limited and 13 related entities for alleged fund diversion of Rs.14.15 crores, fictitious transactions, and misrepresentation of financial statements across FY 2016-17 to 2020-21.

title: SEBI Order Against Mediaone Global Entertainment Ltd. for Fund Diversion and Financial Manipulation
description: SEBI issues enforcement order against Mediaone Global Entertainment Ltd. and its directors for alleged fund diversion and financial irregularities linked to Eros International Media Limited, involving a write-off of ₹63.61 crore.

title: Order in the matter of trading activities of certain entities in the scrip of DU Digital Technologies Limited (now DU Digital Global Limited)
description: SEBI order against 27 entities for employing deceptive trading strategies and contributing to artificial price and volume manipulation in DU Digital scrip during August 26, 2021 to March 31, 2023.

title: Budget 2026 pushes for dedicated REITs to recycle CPSE land assets: Here’s what it means for investors
description: By creating dedicated REITs for CPSE properties, the government aims to monetise high-value real estate, giving investors access to well-located, professionally managed assets with stable tenants and long-term income potential.

title: Will the Budget 2026's tax gambit result in an AI infrastructure boom?
description: A 21-year holiday for cloud providers promises $200 billion in investments, but execution faces constraints in power, water and talent.

title: Budget 2026: Immunity from prosecution, fee for late ITR revision — key income tax rule changes you need to know
description: In keeping with the spirit of Jan Vishwas, ease of living, and to reduce the burden of litigation and compliance, Budget 2026 has proposed changes related to the filing of ITR.

title: Mint Explainer | Budget 2026 telecom receipts: How much is real cash and how much is BSNL accounting?
description: In the Union Budget 2026-27, the government has revised its estimate of telecom receipts up by 71% to  ₹1.4 trillion for 2025-26. But the headline number tells only part of the story.

title: Budget 2026 seeks to make tax regime less adversarial, more predictable: official
description: Through changes to assessment and penalty procedures, alongside selective exemptions and relaxed safe harbour norms, the government is seeking to make the tax system less adversarial and more predictable—both for individuals and for multinational companies.

title: Government unveils support schemes for Divyangjans and sports
description: The 2026 Union budget proposes Divyangjan Kaushal Yojana for skill development in sectors like IT and hospitality and Divyang Sahara Yojana for timely access to assistive devices, supporting persons with disabilities in India.

title: Budget bets on more monitors to tackle India’s pollution crisis
description: The government has proposed a financial outlay of  ₹1,091 crore in the budget for 2026-27 to strengthen monitoring and implementation of pollution control measures across the air, water and noise pollution categories.

title: Mint Explainer | AI mentioned 11 times, but budget cut: Decoding India's evolving stance on artificial intelligence
description: India's AI Mission budget was reduced to  ₹1,000 crore, signaling a cautious stance on AI development. Despite FM Sitharaman's emphasis on AI's potential, the government focuses on sector-specific applications rather than large-scale investments, reflecting changing priorities in the AI landscape.

title: Budget signals shift in export policy, phases out interest subsidies
description: The budget documents show that older interest subsidy schemes have been phased out, while funding is being redirected towards export insurance, trade enforcement and streamlined export support programmes.

title: STT hike: Why derivative traders will dump futures for options
description: While costs to trade both options and futures will rise after the STT hike, overall trading expense in options will be half that of futures, resulting in a likely shift towards options.

title: Budget gives 'Make in India' aircraft a push, scraps customs duty on key components
description: The proposed customs duty exemption on aircraft components in the budget is anticipated to lower costs for manufacturers and airlines, support the growth of the aviation sector, and enhance India's capabilities in aircraft maintenance and production.

title: Union Budget 2026: Clear tax signals, fewer grey zones
description: Budget 2026 avoids headline tax cuts, choosing clarity, certainty and structural reform—reshaping buy-back taxation, MAT, GIFT City incentives and compliance to align tax outcomes with long-term growth.

title: Word cloud: How Budget speeches have changed over the past 10 years
description: An analysis of a decade of Budget speeches shows how the government’s priorities have evolved with changing economic and global conditions.

title: Budget 2026 decoded: Where the money goes, where the risks remain
description: Unlike last year, Budget 2026—the ninth presented by Nirmala Sitharaman—avoids big-bang moves, opting instead for calibrated steps to support growth, avoid the middle-income trap, and stay on track towards developed-nation status.

title: India moves from deficit to a debt-based fiscal pillar to tackle external shocks
description: The new approach comes after the Centre sharply reduced its fiscal deficit from pandemic-era highs, and at a time of rising global uncertainty driven by trade disruptions, geopolitical risks and volatile capital flows.

title: Budget allocates  ₹18,000 crore for power distribution reform
description: India's power distribution companies achieved a net profit in FY25 after significant losses in previous years. The government plans to invest  ₹18,000 crore in FY27 to further support reforms, including the RDSS scheme, despite ongoing financial issues within the sector.

title: TReDS, a new fund, and a top-up: What MSMEs get from the Union budget
description: The Budget's goodies for the MSME sector include a dedicated  ₹10,000 crore SME Growth Fund to create future ‘Champions’, a top-up to the Self-Reliant India Fund with  ₹2,000 crore; and reforms to the TReDS platform.

title: Budget 2026: A snapshot of government finances — where the rupee comes from and goes
description: The Union Budget 2026-27, delivered by Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman, covers key sectors and outlines revenue sources such as taxes and borrowings. The budget highlights government spending on state taxes, interest payments, and defence, among other allocations.

title: Budget 2026: Govt to set up Nimhans 2 to boost mental health support
description: Budget also proposes to upgrade National Mental Health Institutes in Ranchi and Tezpur as regional apex institutions

title: Budget FY27 unveils plans to scale up textiles as export and job engine
description: The textile sector is crucial as a major foreign exchange earner and employment generator for India. The country exported textile and apparel exports worth $36.60 billion in FY25, underscoring the sector’s importance as a foreign exchange earner and major employment generator.

title: Sindoor effect: India's capital outlay for defence jumps 18% to  ₹2.2 trillion
description: India has raised its defence capital outlay by 18% to nearly  ₹2.2 trillion for FY27, aiming to modernize military assets amid security threats. The budget allocates funds for advanced weapons and equipment, while emphasizing self-reliance in defence manufacturing.

title: Budget allows voluntary declaration of foreign assets
description: The government is shifting away from the ‘one size fits all’ punitive approach of the Black Money Act, offering a tiered amnesty for assets up to  ₹5 crore to reduce litigation.

title: Budget makes a targeted push to drive manufacturing, exports
description: A key plank of the export push is targeted customs relief for labour-intensive sectors. The Budget also uses customs policy as a tool to strengthen domestic manufacturing capabilities in strategic sectors.

title: Budget 2026 restricts tax exemption on sovereign gold bonds
description: The capital gains tax exemption on SGBs will only be available to original subscribers holding these bonds till maturity. Others who buy SGBs from stock exchanges or other holders will have to pay tax.

title: Budget 2026 ‘tactical’ but not a ‘breakthrough’: What Moody's said about India's fiscal consolidation, deficit
description: Moody's Ratings has termed India's Union Budget tactical but not groundbreaking, noting that fiscal consolidation won't alter the country's credit profile.&nbsp;

title: How poll-bound states stand to gain from Budget despite no specific proposals for them
description: By prioritizing regional initiatives like rare earth corridors, chemical parks, and coconut cultivation schemes, the government addresses specific economic needs while maintaining a nationally applied development strategy.

title: Govt proposes secondary market for MSME invoices via TReDS securitization
description: The Union Budget for 2026-27 has proposed to treat TReDS receivables as asset-backed securities, aiming to boost liquidity and widen funding options for small businesses.

title: Budget 2026: Push for high-value crops, funds for agri research slashed
description: There was little for farmers battling a crash in crop prices and incomes, and funds for agri research were slashed amid growing climate risks and a productive crisis.

title: Balancing capex and deficits in an era of coconuts, factories and high-speed trains
description: Nirmala Sitharaman's Union budget emphasizes reducing public debt and achieving fiscal targets despite lower tax revenues. The budget includes strategic investments in domestic production and infrastructure, addressing geopolitical threats and aiming for economic growth.

title: Govt's  ₹60k-cr extra telecom receipt aim dims Airtel's AGR relief chance
description: Telecom analysts question where this extra  ₹60,000 crore is going to come from for the communications ministry, as it has already shown an earning of about  ₹80,000 crore as of December end from sources such as licence fee from private telecom operators.

title: Longer tax holiday, lower post-holiday tax: How IFSC units benefit from the Union Budget
description: The tax holiday for companies operating from IFSC has been doubled to 20 years. Besides, they will pay a lower tax of 15% once the tax holiday ends, compared to the current rates of 25-35%.

title: PM Internship Scheme sees sharp cut in funding in Budget 2026 amid poor response
description: The Union Budget for FY27 has slashed funding for the PM Internship Scheme following poor response, with an allocation of  ₹4,788 crore, compared to over  ₹10,000 crore budgeted for in FY26.

title: Budget proposes 15.5% minimum profit for taxing foreign tech units
description: The Union Budget for 2026-27 has proposed a single tax framework for global capability centres, ending years of ambiguity over how their IT services work is taxed.

title: Budget 2026 brings Total Return Swaps to corporate bonds: What this means for India's market
description: The government introduced initiatives to deepen India's corporate bond market, including total return swaps (TRS) and incentives for municipalities to raise funds. But experts suggest TRS may primarily benefit foreign investors and is unlikely to create sticky, long-term demand for corporate bonds.
